sourceObservation: Derived from the usage-warning rendered sample: object header, threshold bar, two compact metrics, and a bottom control/support action.
layoutSummary: Header object, horizontal adaptive Progress bar, two side-by-side metrics, bottom action/support pill.
allowedEdits: bind Progress value, total, and color to DataModel paths
replace Progress value and total with proven-safe numeric values only for static mock data
add onClick to controlAction only with valid event capability

adaptiveProgressGuidance.valueModel: For usage-limit cards, set /progress/total to limitMinutes. If usedMinutes is within limit, set /progress/value to usedMinutes. If usedMinutes exceeds limit, clamp /progress/value to limitMinutes so Progress renders full; expose the overage only as text such as metric.2.value.
adaptiveProgressGuidance.runtimeBinding: Recompute /usage/overMinutes = max(usedMinutes - limitMinutes, 0), /progress/value = min(usedMinutes, limitMinutes), /progress/total = max(limitMinutes, 1), and /progress/color whenever usage or limit changes. Do not calculate or bind visual widths for this template.
adaptiveProgressGuidance.colors: Use green or teal for within-limit usage and rose/red for over-limit usage. The over-limit state may turn the Progress color red, while metric text continues to carry the exact exceeded duration.
adaptiveProgressGuidance.structure: Use a single linear Progress component inside the usageBar slot. Do not add Stack overlays or segmented bars unless the user explicitly asks to visualize the exceeded proportion.

forbiddenEdits: use this as a generic decorative bar
duplicate the same time value in both metrics
calculate visible bar widths from usage durations
represent exceeded duration as a proportional extra segment
deleteRules: If one metric is not real, merge metricsRow into one centered metric; if threshold no longer drives the layout, abandon this template.

colorPolicy.budget: dark neutral surface plus one real status/action signal

roles.panel: comp_background_secondary dark glass (#19ffffff) with font_on_tertiary border
roles.progress: DataModel color must be a token-backed status/action color such as confirm or brand

rewriteRules: Do not introduce a second high-saturation foreground on the dark surface.
Use warning/alert only when the quota is truly warning/alert state.
